evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Model name 'claude-opus-4-8' appears fabricated (no such Anthropic model identifier exists; real names follow patterns like claude-opus-4-20250514 or claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022). Also invents SDK classes 'AnthropicBedrockMantle', 'AnthropicFoundry', 'AnthropicAWS' that don't exist in the anthropic-python SDK. Cache_control structure on tools/system and tool_use flow patterns are otherwise plausible.
evidence.executability: Uses 'from fastapi.sse import EventSourceResponse' — this module does not exist in FastAPI; EventSourceResponse comes from sse-starlette package. Imports anthropic_client from app.main creating circular import risk with router modules. Model string would 404. Multiple files spread across modules without a single runnable entry; extractor would write divergent files.
evidence.completeness: (a) API key acquisition mentioned but no URL to console.anthropic.com; (b) install command present in bash; (c) .env template present (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY); (d) backend code present but split across ~10 implicit file paths; (e) no frontend (FastAPI-only stack, acceptable); (f) test commands shown as pytest files but no explicit 'pytest' run command in a final test block. Duplicate content across sections.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Uses numbered bracket citations [1][6][7] etc. with no resolvable URLs. No canonical docs.anthropic.com URLs placed near code. Citations are opaque footnote markers, not links.

rubric_notes: docs_unavailable=true; applied long-tail handling. Internal consistency reasonable but fabricated model identifier 'claude-opus-4-8' (repeated throughout) and invented SDK class names hurt correctness. fastapi.sse.EventSourceResponse is a real bug — that import path does not exist.
